description: capital of and state in Austria
description_wiki: Vienna (  vee-EN-ə; German: Wien [viːn] ; Austro-Bavarian: Wean [veɐ̯n]) is the capital, most populous city, and one of the nine states of Austria.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description_wiki: Hungary is a landlocked country in Central Europe.

description_wiki: The State Academy of Fine Arts Stuttgart (German: Staatliche Akademie der bildenden Künste Stuttgart, or ABK Stuttgart) is a public fine art university in Stuttgart, Germany.

description: German architect and designer (1883-1960)
description_wiki: Fritz August Breuhaus (February 9, 1883 – December 2, 1960) was a German architect, interior designer, and designer in the 20th century.

description: most populous city in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany
description_wiki: Cologne (  kə-LOHN; German: Köln [kœln] ; Kölsch: Kölle [ˈkœ̂lə] ) is the fourth-most populous city of Germany and the largest city of the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia with nearly 1.1 million inhabitants in the city proper and over 3.1 million people in the Cologne Bonn urban region.

description_wiki: Wilmersdorf (German pronunciation: [ˈvɪlmɐsdɔʁf] ) is an inner-city locality of Berlin which lies south-west of the central city.

description: city on the Neckar river and capital of the federal state Baden-Württemberg, Germany
description_wiki: Stuttgart (; German: [ˈʃtʊtɡaʁt] ; Swabian: Schduagert [ˈʃd̥ua̯ɡ̊ɛʕd̥]; Alemannic: Stuttgart; names in other languages) is the capital and largest city of the German state of Baden-Württemberg.

description: housing estate built for exhibition in Stuttgart in 1927 showcasing International style architecture
description_wiki: The Weissenhof Estate (German: Weißenhofsiedlung, pronounced [ˈvaɪsn̩hoːfˌziːdlʊŋ]) is a housing estate built for the 1927 Deutscher Werkbund exhibition in Stuttgart, Germany.

description: capital city of the German federated state of North Rhine-Westphalia
description_wiki: Düsseldorf (German: [ˈdʏsl̩dɔʁf] ) is the capital city of North Rhine-Westphalia, the most populous state of Germany.
